info.title: Harness NextGen Software Delivery Platform API Reference
info.description: This is the Open Api Spec 3 for the Access Control Service. This is under active development. Beware of the breaking change with respect to the generated code stub.

tags.name: Account Roles
tags.description: This contains APIs for performing CRUD operations on Account level Roles.

tags.name: Organization Roles
tags.description: This contains APIs for performing CRUD operations on Organization level Roles.

tags.name: Project Roles
tags.description: This contains APIs for performing CRUD operations on Project level Roles.

get.description: Returns a list of Roles present in the Account scope.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pagination page number strategy: Specify the page number within the paginated collection related to the number of items on each page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination: Number of items to return.

parameters.name: search_term
parameters.description: This would be used to filter resources having attributes matching the search term.

parameters.name: Harness-Account
parameters.description: Slug field of the account the resource is scoped to. This is required for Authorization methods other than the x-api-key header. If you are using the x-api-key header, this can be skipped.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Parameter on the basis of which sorting is done.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Order on the basis of which sorting is done.

Link.description: Links to related resources, in the format defined by [RFC 5988](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-5). This will include a link with relation type `next` to the next page, if there is a next page.

allowed_scope_levels.description: The Scope levels at which this Role can be used.

harness_managed.description: This indicates if this Role is managed by Harness or not. If true, Harness can manage and modify this Role.

x-api-key.description: API key is a token provided while making the API calls. This is used to authenticate the client at the exposed endpoint.
